description: A foundational document defining the Grand Theft Auto game development project's purpose, objectives, high-level requirements, success criteria, and authority structure. It establishes the project's scope, budget ($500M USD), timeline (Q4 2029 release), and key stakeholder roles. This document serves as the formal authorization and reference point for all subsequent planning activities.
steps_to_create: Convene project leadership including publisher representatives, executive sponsors, and key department heads
Define project vision, objectives, and strategic alignment with the GTA franchise
Establish high-level scope, deliverables, and exclusions
Document budget allocation ($500M USD) and major cost categories
Outline project timeline and key milestones (Q2 2026 pre-production, Q4 2027 alpha, Q2 2029 beta, Q3 2029 gold master, Q4 2029 launch)
Define success metrics and acceptance criteria
Identify key stakeholders and their roles
Secure executive sponsor sign-off and formal authorization
approval_authorities: Executive Sponsor, Publisher Representative, Chief Financial Officer

description: A comprehensive framework document identifying all material risks (technical, financial, operational, regulatory, supply chain, security, and social) across the game development lifecycle. For each risk, it documents risk description, probability assessment, impact severity, mitigation strategies with specific SMART actions, responsible owners, and monitoring mechanisms. This framework operationalizes the high-level risks identified in the project plan with concrete, measurable mitigation activities and assigns accountability.
steps_to_create: Conduct a comprehensive risk identification workshop with cross-functional team (technical leads, financial officer, operations, compliance)

Categorize risks by domain: technical (procedural generation integration, performance optimization, AI bias), financial (funding partnership failures, cost overruns), operational (multi-location team coordination, personnel turnover), regulatory (content restrictions, GDPR/CCPA compliance), supply chain (GPU procurement, component availability), security (data breaches, IP theft), and social (content controversies, stakeholder conflicts)
Assign probability and impact ratings to each identified risk using a standard matrix
Develop specific, SMART mitigation strategies for high-priority risks with clear ownership and timelines
Establish key risk indicators (KRIs) and monitoring mechanisms for ongoing risk tracking
Define escalation procedures and contingency responses for each critical risk
approval_authorities: Risk and Compliance Officer, Project Manager, Executive Sponsor
